article_title: Google Adds Reusable AI Skills to Chrome, Turning Gemini Prompts Into One-Click Workflows Across Tabs

content_preview.title: Google Adds Reusable AI Skills to Chrome, Turning Gemini Prompts Into One-Click Workflows Across Tabs
content_preview.summary: Chrome's new Skills feature lets users save Gemini prompts and run them across web pages with a single click, rolling out on desktop with a pre-built library of common workflows.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Google is rolling out a new feature called Skills to Gemini in Chrome that turns frequently used AI prompts into saved, reusable workflows that can be triggered with a single click across any web page. The feature, announced on April 14 by Chrome product manager Hafsah Ismail, represents a shift from conversational AI interaction toward embedded, task-oriented automation inside the browser, according to the [Google blog](https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/chrome/ski...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong consumer tech article on Google Skills in Chrome feature.
editor_notes.source_verification: Google blog VERIFIED via WebFetch: Skills in Chrome, one-click reusable prompts, pre-built library (protein macros, spec comparison), / or + activation, confirmation for sensitive actions, Mac/Windows/ChromeOS desktop, English-US, compass icon management all confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All feature details verified against Google primary source.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Google Chrome Skills coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid consumer AI feature article. Approved.
